report_name: German Intelligence (BND) Warns Businesses of Russian Cyber Threats After Official Targeted in Phishing Campaign

threat_actors.name: Russian state-sponsored actors
threat_actors.description: A Russian state-sponsored threat actor group that conducted a sophisticated phishing campaign via secure messaging apps (Signal, WhatsApp) against high-value European targets, including a senior BND official, diplomats, politicians, and journalists.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering, economic espionage, and influence operations.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors delivered malicious links through targeted messages on secure messaging apps like Signal and WhatsApp.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Identity Information
attack_patterns.description: The attackers conducted reconnaissance to identify and profile high-value targets, including intelligence agents, diplomats, politicians, and journalists across Europe.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing for Information: Spearphishing via Service
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors used third-party secure messaging services like Signal and WhatsApp to conduct the phishing attack against targeted individuals.

tools.name: Signal
tools.description: Used as a vector for a phishing campaign to send malicious messages to targeted individuals.

tools.name: WhatsApp
tools.description: Used as a vector for a phishing campaign to send malicious messages to targeted individuals.

identities.name: Bundesnachrichtendienst (BND)
identities.description: Germany's foreign intelligence service, a senior official of which was compromised in a Russian phishing campaign.

identities.name: German business leaders
identities.description: Warned by the BND about the heightened risk of Russian cyberattacks for economic espionage.

identities.name: European officials
identities.description: A target group for the Russian phishing campaign.

identities.name: Diplomats
identities.description: A target group for the Russian phishing campaign.

identities.name: Journalists
identities.description: A target group for the Russian phishing campaign.

infrastructure.name: Phishing Delivery Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used to host and deliver malicious links via secure messaging apps like Signal and WhatsApp.

indicators.name: Potential Phishing Link via URL Shortener
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Phishing Link via URL Shortener
id: 2b9c1d8e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic to common URL shortening services. These services can be used to obfuscate malicious links in phishing campaigns, such as the one targeting European officials via secure messaging apps.
references:
  - https://www.intelligenceonline.com/europe-russia/2026/06/04/bnd-warns-business-leaders-of-russian-threat,110780676-bre
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-04
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - 'bit.ly'
      - 't.co'
      - 'tinyurl.com'
      - 'is.gd'
      - 'soo.gd'
      - 'short.io'
      - 'rebrand.ly'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects traffic to common URL shortening services, which can be used to obfuscate malicious links in phishing campaigns, as seen in the campaign targeting European officials.

relationships.target_name: Phishing for Information: Spearphishing via Service

relationships.source_name: Potential Phishing Link via URL Shortener
